title: Joseon Dynasty Quiz
subtitle: Founders, Hangul, and Joseon history
primer:
The Joseon Dynasty (1392-1910) was one of the longest-ruling dynasties in world history, governing the Korean peninsula for more than 500 years. Founded by General Yi Seong-gye, later known as King Taejo, Joseon replaced the Goryeo Kingdom and established Neo-Confucianism as the guiding philosophy of government and society.

Joseon is also remembered for major cultural achievements, especially under King Sejong the Great. Sejong sponsored scientific advances and created Hangul, the Korean alphabet, which made literacy more accessible to ordinary people. The dynasty's political structure, scholarship, and cultural legacy still shape Korean identity today.

trivia_questions.question: Who was the founder of the Joseon dynasty and what was his position?
trivia_questions.explanation: Yi Seong-gye, also known as King Taejo, founded the Joseon dynasty in 1392.

trivia_questions.question: Is the year the Joseon dynasty began odd or even?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Joseon dynasty began in 1392, which is an even number.

curated_sources.title: Joseon Dynasty — Britannica

trivia_questions.question: How many years did the Joseon dynasty last?
trivia_questions.explanation: The Joseon dynasty lasted from 1392 to 1910, which is 518 years.
